page: Amazon Web Services r8a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, scoring in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression. Multi-core performance, database operations, and memory bandwidth tests generally fall into the average category. For specialized workloads, the CPU supports advanced vector extensions (AVX-512) and cryptography instructions, yielding top-tier results in small-model LLM inference and image processing. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and a moderate 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth represent potential bottlenecks for I/O-heavy applications.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8a.4xlarge provides a high density of DDR5 memory relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for memory-bound applications without the overhead of higher-tier compute instances. It is appropriate for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, text and image processing pipelines, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r8a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. While the instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, it provides a baseline network speed of 8 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU execution, single-threaded decompression, and small-model LLM inference, while multi-core operations and memory bandwidth perform at average levels. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. It is well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, text processing, and moderate data-science tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core benchmark performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86 platform featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.

128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (8.0 GB per core)
